title: Level 317
wikitext:

My foolishness The Lord shows no mercy A wall of tangled grass Like a prison I move forward with this Seeing no light Seeing only despair I struggle to think What is the end of my path What should be the end of my path A road without an ending Like euthanasia I once believed this was merely a task In truth, there is no task at all Only a road leading to the abyss Ahead is still darkness There is no food left Blood flows Yet there is no life If I could return to the beginning I would honor this place I would leave this place Now, I sell my soul In unity with this land If there are those who come after Hear my telling Do not rest Do not believe May you be able to escape this place
oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will oh I will My favorite thing is maze
{{Blurquote|image=File=mcscdsas1.jpg|quote-text=When I was very young, I already loved mazes. Weaving my way through them has always been a great part of my life. During those early years, I would repeatedly beg the only pillar of my existence—my mother—to take me to those places. I could almost memorize every path in every maze; even the strangest dead ends tucked away along the edges could not escape my eyes.}} {{Class 2|class=4e - Environmental|security=Unstable|entity=Several Non-Entity Hazards|hexagon=4}} __NOTOC__ __NOEDITSECTION__ [[Category:Levels]][[Category:Format Screws]][[Category:Levels]] '''Level 317''', also known as "Royal Gardens", is the 318th level of the Backrooms. {{Blurquote|image=mscdcas1.jpg|quote-text=Within the barriers of a maze, I always felt as though the flow of time itself had fallen into stillness. All the noise and worries of the outside world were gently shut out by thick walls. The only mission entrusted to me was to converse with this enormous geometric riddle. That task was profoundly delightful—an indescribable joy that filled me completely. I even once tried to confide in the birds circling above the maze. Thinking back now, it feels rather silly; they could not understand me at all.}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Maze image 2.jpg|caption=}} Level 317 manifests as an infinitely extending hedge maze. The pathways are made of packed dirt and appear extremely dry. Time in Level 317 seems permanently fixed in the afternoon, and the weather is always clear. The maze appears to be infinite in scale, with Euclidean and non-Euclidean spaces interwoven at random, and the walls constantly shifting positions. There are an extremely large number of dead ends; at the end of some of them, several bottles of almond water can be found, possibly generated by the level itself. The maze walls are composed of plant leaves intertwined with vines and tree branches of varying lengths. Longer branches serve as obstacles within the level, blocking wanderers from passing. The thickness of the walls is difficult to measure. Attempts to cut or dig into the hedges reveal an internal depth far exceeding what their exterior proportions would suggest. Many records indicate that penetrating the interior of the hedges leads to another parallel corridor system, whose orientation is completely misaligned with the main area. Anyone attempting to climb upward will be killed by suddenly extending and retracting branches, which specifically target the heart and brainstem. Artistic structures commonly appear in wider sections of the maze. Without exception, these take the form of marble sculptures, shattered stained-glass windows, or illogical geometric metal constructs. Strangely, despite the dry environment, these artworks remain in pristine condition, showing no signs of dust accumulation or weathering. Occasionally, pools of water also appear. These pools typically take regular geometric shapes, such as circles, hexagons, or elongated rectangles, with edges made of marble identical to that of the sculptures. The water is crystal clear, yet its depth cannot be accurately measured by any means. Objects thrown into the water usually disappear silently, producing no splash. Some explorers claim that after staring at the water’s surface for more than three minutes, the reflection begins to lag behind reality or displays a maze layout different from the actual surroundings. {{Blurquote|image=maservyim1.png|quote-text=After adulthood arrived, I became independent and built a life of my own. I traveled to many countries to seek out and satisfy my fervent passion for mazes. Yet the United Kingdom—my homeland—remained the finest of them all. The intricate labyrinths there captivated me, and the leaves were impossibly green. Every holiday, I would wander into a maze, admiring the flowers, the plants, and countless other small details—each possessing its own unique beauty. As the years passed, the towering walls that once rose above my head no longer seemed like obstacles.}} ==Parallel Corridor System== {{Blurimage|file=Mazeeeeee.jpg|caption=}} The parallel corridor system is the first sub-area of Level 317. All of its corridors are geometrically projected in parallel alignment with the original main area. However, due to the region’s unique spatial phase, the two are physically non-connecting; that is, an observer within one space is absolutely unable to perceive the existence of the other by any means. Wanderers may be fatally injured by sudden spatial shearing as a result of this phenomenon. The pathways within The parallel corridor system are composed of a mixture of concrete and gravel, covered with artificial turf that remains in a constant state of dampness. The corridors are extremely narrow, requiring wanderers to move sideways in order to pass through. The plant-covered walls feel sticky and cold to the touch, and are suspected to contain toxic sap. Art installations are far less common here than in the main area. In their place are numerous spaces that appear to be open ground. In reality, stepping onto these areas causes the individual to fall into a water well approximately five meters deep. The interior walls of these wells are perfectly smooth, making escape impossible without tools. Within The parallel corridor system, ticking clock sounds may occasionally be heard. The volume of these sounds increases over time relative to the individual wanderer’s duration of presence. The decibel level is personalized—each individual experiences their own distinct intensity. Traveling deep enough into The parallel corridor system will eventually lead to another new sub-area. {{Blurquote|image=masetgv1.jpg|quote-text=I once wondered what other hobbies I might have developed. Perhaps I would shoulder a heavy DSLR camera and stand alone by the sea, capturing the final shimmer of sunset light. Or perhaps I would linger beside a smoke-filled billiards table, meticulously calculating the parabolic trajectory of each collision, attempting to exhaust what remained of my innocence in those pure diversions. Yet whenever such thoughts surfaced, a faint, mocking laugh would echo from deep within me. I knew that kind of life would be far too pale and ordinary for me. Only the maze was destined to intertwine with my fate—the sole companion with whom I would share the rest of my days.}} ==Deep Passage== {{Blurimage|file=jtytj.jpg|caption=}} The deep passage is the second sub-area of Level 317. Its corridor width is nearly identical to that of the previous sub-area; however, the passages are winding rather than straight, and may have completely detached from the main space in an objective sense. The plant matter forming the walls is replaced here by a green fibrous mass resembling vegetation. These fibers are tightly interwoven, hard as iron, and continuously emit a scent resembling a mixture of aged book pages and lavender. The artistic structures in the deep passage are severely distorted. Marble sculptures appear in states of melting and fusion, like wax figures exposed to extreme heat, entangled with the green fibers of the walls to form twisted humanoid obstacles. Any attempt to touch these structures results in death, specifically by dissolution. The deep passage remains predominantly under clear skies. However, there is a low probability of the appearance of a shadow band. This phenomenon is not caused by clouds blocking sunlight, but rather serves as an indication of spatial dimensional folding occurring in the area. During a shadow band event, all light rapidly recedes, and once the phenomenon concludes, the maze’s layout undergoes drastic reconfiguration. Remaining within the deep passage for a prolonged period—ranging from one to two months—will result in a transition to a new sub-area. It should be noted that from this sub-area onward, documentation becomes extremely scarce. {{Blurquote|image=Merwhas1.webp|quote-text=One day, I was walking along a sidewalk in London. It wasn’t raining—an unusual day for the city. I was holding a coffee I had bought from Costa, a very common brand. As I took a sip, my foot caught on something, and I fell forward. When I opened my eyes, I found myself in a strange yellow hall. Where was this place? All I could see was endless yellow wallpaper and dim fluorescent lights. For a moment, I felt lost. I realized I might have arrived somewhere that did not belong to Earth, which meant I might never see a maze again.}} ==Stifled Zone== {{Blurimage|file=meeeeeee.jpg|caption=}} The stifled zone is the third sub-area. Visually, its corridors appear wide once more, but in actual spatial terms, the area exists in a semi-collapsed state. Reports indicate that wanderers can directly sense the boundaries of space becoming indistinct, with walls and floors occasionally trembling like ripples across the surface of water. The fibrous walls here are covered in crystalline formations. Although they still mimic the trimmed shapes of hedges, their surfaces are smooth. Due to the extremely high reflectivity of the crystalline walls, sunlight repeatedly refracts within the narrow corridors, creating countless false projections of pathways. The stifled zone is extremely hot, with temperatures exceeding 50°C. As the walls are no longer fibrous but crystalline, moisture is entirely absent. Sweat rapidly evaporates under the intense reflected light, causing wanderers to easily fall into severe dehydration and heat exhaustion without realizing it. Fragments resembling withered hedge leaves occasionally litter the ground. In reality, these are flakes that have broken off from the crystalline walls and been scorched by the high temperatures, forming thin, razor-sharp sheets. Wanderers wearing thin-soled footwear are highly susceptible to cuts. These wounds do not bleed; instead, moisture is immediately absorbed by the crystalline fibers, resulting in dry fissures that are difficult to heal. By definition, the stifled zone contains no artistic installations. All materials that once formed such constructs have shattered and now float as fragments in midair. If a fully intact sculpture is encountered, the wanderer will be transported to the next sub-area. The probability of this occurring is considered extremely low. {{Blurquote|image=mwas1.jpg|quote-text=In order to escape this nightmare, I began frantically searching for an exit within that boundless yellow space. Until one day, I happened upon a heavy iron hammer lying on the damp floor. Summoning all my strength, I smashed through the wall and was astonished to discover a gray, peculiar corridor hidden behind it, lined on both sides with countless doors. Although the nauseating stench of damp carpet still permeated the air, I felt that, at last, there was hope.}} ==New Open ExpanseThis is because the main area is known as the open expanse.== {{Blurimage|file=meeeezxe.jpg|caption=}} The new open expanse is the fourth sub-area. According to records, the maze corridors here are the same width as those in the main area. Visually, the new open expanse appears exceptionally clean: the walls revert to vibrant green hedges, and the ground consists of flat, dry, gray-white soil. Like the previous sub-area, this region is dry, though the temperature is reduced to approximately 40°C. Spatial distortion remains present in the new open expanse. The vanishing point in the distance appears to be stretched infinitely far away. When looking forward, a corridor may seem to extend for several kilometers, yet in reality, a turn will be reached in less than ten meters—conversely, short-looking paths may take far longer to traverse. The new open expanse is filled with a greater number of maze-themed artistic works than the main area, particularly humanoid sculptures. These artworks do not physically move; however, if they possess sculpted features resembling eyes or other observational elements, they will track the wanderer’s movement. Resting within the new open expanse triggers crystalline growths similar to those of the previous sub-area, emerging radially from beneath the individual. If one does not immediately relocate, the wanderer will be encased within seconds, transformed into an intricate humanoid art piece. After prolonged exposure to the new open expanse, auditory perception gradually dulls, eventually resulting in the inability to hear any sound despite being able to see one’s own movements. This condition is irreversible, even through the use of almond water. If the corridors of the new open expanse become perfectly straight, this indicates entry into the fifth sub-area. {{Blurquote|image=mafdss1.jpg|quote-text=I continued arriving at different places. One of them, beyond the corridor, was a bizarre space submerged in water. The surface was murky, tinted with an unsettling brown—as if soil and foul wastewater had merged into a putrid liquid. There, I finally saw other people. Yet I did not speak a single word to them.}} ==Faceted Grounds== {{Blurimage|file=rrrrrr.jpg|caption=}} The faceted grounds is the fifth sub-area. The corridor width continues from the previous sub-area, but the environment regains a damp quality and the corridors return to a straight configuration. The ground appears to be paved with a yellowish metallic material that conducts heat rapidly; direct contact with the floor is strongly discouraged. The walls of the faceted grounds take on a geometric, angular form. The hedges are cut into distinct polyhedral structures, each face precisely angled with sharp edges. Sunlight creates stark divisions of light and shadow across their surfaces, giving the entire area an almost black-and-white visual contrast. Unlike the crystalline reflections of the stifled zone, the reflections here are softer; however, the shadows are abnormally deep, and no light source can penetrate the shaded regions. Numerous misaligned fissures exist throughout the faceted grounds. These gaps are only a few centimeters wide, yet appear infinitely deep. Objects dropped into them produce no echo or impact sound. When light is directed inside, the beam disappears within three seconds. Any attempt to insert a hand into a fissure is strongly discouraged. Reports indicate that upon contact, a wanderer’s arm may undergo proportional distortion, with the number of fingers temporarily increasing or decreasing before returning to normal several minutes later. Resting within the faceted grounds will still trigger crystalline attacks, emerging from either the left or right side. The consequence is assimilation into the wall structure itself. Furthermore, these attacks appear to cause a chain reaction; survivors report that nearby wanderers are also subjected to the same event. There are no known artistic installations within the faceted grounds. Only a few pavilions have been observed. These structures are composed of straw, with triangular roofs supported by four pillars. Given the hazards present in this area, they are presumed to be traps. Ordinary plant specimens may appear independently, initially displaying multiple colors. Over time, their numbers steadily increase, while their coloration gradually shifts until only purple remains. When this occurs, transition into the next sub-area will follow. {{Blurquote|image=masrr1.jpg|quote-text=Opened a glass door and left that watery place me. Next came a hall full of mirrors, then a tunnel that seemed to be filled with carbon dioxide, then a snow bunker, and right after that… a hell. I have no idea how I managed to leave it—perhaps through sheer willpower, or perhaps by luck alone.}} ==Kafkaesque== {{Blurimage|file=Level -2new.png|caption=}} Kafkaesque is the sixth sub-area. It consists of a vast network of hedge maze corridors formed from purple foliage, with the ground also entirely purple. Additionally, the ground possesses strong sound-absorbing properties, resulting in a state of absolute silence throughout the area. Numerous wooden office desks obstructThe emergence of these things is likely due to the names the sub-area was given. Initially, there was no evidence of their existence. the corridors in Kafkaesque. These desks may appear piled together, sometimes blocking passages entirely and turning them into dead ends. Opening desk drawers at random typically reveals mountains of blank forms, broken typewriter components, or dried purple wisteria petals. Distant castle-like structures can be seen from within Kafkaesque. Through binoculars, the texture of the stone bricks is clearly visible; however, it is impossible to approach these buildings under any circumstances. They appear to be a form of anomalous visual illusion. Within Kafkaesque, wanderers lose all sense of direction, including vertical orientation. There is currently no known method to counteract this effect. This condition may result in psychological collapse. In Kafkaesque, any item other than one’s clothing will disappear. This makes survival in the area absolutely unfeasible, and the likelihood of death is extremely high. Kafkaesque also contains random blue spheres. Touching them will result in teleportation to a random location within the sub-area, or, in rarer cases, to the next sub-area. Only two such cases of the latter outcome have been documented. {{Blurquote|image=masr1.jpg|quote-text=When I finally escaped that horrific place and found myself standing before a convenience store beside a forest, I at last broke my long silence. The person I spoke to claimed he too had accidentally fallen into this realm. By coincidence, he was born in the same country as I was. Later he emigrated to Czechia, though he occasionally returned home. He mentioned that his favorite memory was the time he visited a maze. Wow—it had been so long since I had heard anyone speak of mazes. It felt unimaginably distant.}} ==Farway== {{Blurimage|file=yyyy.jpg|caption=}} Farway is the seventh sub-area and currently the last known sub-area. However, as previously stated, only two entry cases have ever been recorded—one individual is confirmed deceased, and the other is missing. As a result, the available documentation on this area is extremely limited, and there may be unknown inaccuracies in existing reports. Farway is scattered with fragments of stained glass and suspended droplets of water, both in unstable states. Reports indicate that wanderers can see visions of their own deaths reflected within these surfaces. Whether these visions depict inevitable outcomes or mere simulations remains unknown. The walls of Farway are believed to be purple as well, though the coloration appears profoundly unnatural. All photographs taken within the area display distortion, warping, and monochrome coloration. One theory suggests that the purple of Farway is not truly a color, but rather a projection of higher-dimensional energy. The human brain, in order to prevent consciousness from being overwhelmed by this unnatural spectrum, forcibly interprets it as purple. However, electronic imaging sensors are unable to process such a projection accurately. Farway contains strange, twisted pipe-like structures crawling across the ground. These pipes feel similar in texture to ordinary garden hoses, though they possess notable rigidity. Damaging these pipes causes polluted liquid to spray outward. The substance exhibits either highly acidic or highly alkaline properties and can rapidly corrode exposed skin. At present, there are no records of any further sub-areas beyond Farway. It is considered possible that this is the final sub-area. {{Blurquote|image=mas1f.jpg|quote-text=Time rushed forward amid these surreal landscapes. I passed through a vast drive-in theater, a blue district drenched in the aesthetics of the 80s, a silent and frigid underground city, and a house. Then I encountered a place I had never imagined I would see again after arriving in this surreal world—a place I had never dared to hope I would reunite with. It was steeped in nostalgia.}} ==Entities== No entities have been documented within the level. ==Colonies & Outposts== No entities have been outposts or colonies within the level. ==Entrances== *Passing through most mazes in the Backrooms will result in a transition to this location. *Digging sandpits within [[Level 50]] grants a chance of being transported here. ==Exits== *The method of exiting Level 317 is via an abrupt transition. The probability remains unknown. *One theory suggests that reaching the “end” of the maze leads to [[Level 173]]. However, since no one has ever reached this potentially existing location, it cannot be confirmed. {{footnotes}} {{Blurquote|image=mas1.jpg|quote-text=When I saw the maze, I was stunned, though it carried a strange aura. To be honest, I am standing inside it at this very moment. I cannot precisely describe what I feel; I can only say that I am… happy. I believe I will find the exit and leave this place, even if I may feel reluctant—because this may well be both my first and my last time here. Wish me success. And I will succeed.}}

{{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:WikipediaBox|subtext= |title=Adjustment Suggestions:|image=59599.jpg|content= I. Executive Summary
Farway is the seventh known sub-area of Level 317 and, as of the time of this report, the last confirmed sub-area in existence. According to existing records, only two entry cases have been documented: *Case A: Confirmed deceased. *Case B: Lost contact after entry, has not returned, currently listed as missing. Given Farway’s extreme hazard level, scarcity of information, and environmental instability, this report conducts a risk analysis and provides recommendations regarding whether Case B should be reclassified from missing to confirmed deceased. II. Background
2.1. Area Overview
Farway is the seventh sub-area of Level 317. Based on the limited surviving data, its primary characteristics include: *Numerous stained-glass structures scattered throughout the space, along with droplets of unknown origin. *An unstable environmental state, with possible localized spatial distortions. *Walls presumed to be purple in color, though presented in an unnaturally distorted manner. 2.2. Visual and Perceptual Anomalies
Reports indicate that within the stained glass or on the surfaces of the droplets, individuals may witness imagery depicting their own death. It remains undetermined whether such imagery constitutes: *An inevitable future event. *A psychologically induced hallucination or simulation. *A form of higher-dimensional information leakage. Furthermore, all recorded attempts to photograph Farway have resulted in: *Severe distortion *Visual warping *Monochromatic (black-and-white) degradation This phenomenon implies that no reliable visual records currently exist for forensic or search-and-rescue analysis. III. Physical Hazard Assessment
The ground surface of Farway is covered with numerous twisted, pipe-like structures exhibiting the following properties: *Texture similar to a garden hose *Disproportionate hardness *Capable of withstanding pressure, though brittleness remains unknown If stepped on and ruptured, these structures release highly corrosive acidic or alkaline wastewater. Documented effects include: *Instantaneous skin corrosion *Potential deep tissue necrosis *Possible respiratory tract burns in enclosed environments Based on the above hazards, Farway does not meet conventional survivability conditions. IV. Statistical and Risk Modeling Total known entry cases: 2 *Confirmed deaths: 1 *Missing persons: 1 Given the extremely small sample size, the minimum observed mortality rate is 50%. Taking into account: *The area is presumed to be the final sub-area of Level 317 *No recorded instances of return *No documented stable exits *The presence of both psychological and physical lethal factors The projected survival probability approaches statistical zero. V. Administrative Recommendation
According to identity classification standards: If entry into a region entails high lethality and no signs of survival are observed within a reasonable timeframe, the individual may be regarded as confirmed deceased. Farway meets the following criteria: *Highly lethal environment *No feasible search-and-rescue operations *No recoverable evidence *No residual communication signals Therefore, this report recommends: Reclassifying Case B from missing to presumed deceased, with automatic conversion to confirmed deceased status after sixty days. VI. Conclusion
The missing individual should be classified as confirmed deceased. }}





























































{{Image Banner|image=mazerglo.jpg}} Where are you standing right now? On that stretch of mud that never seems to dry, or in that narrow gap where you have to turn sideways, feeling the sticky fluid from the walls seep into your clothes? I wouldn’t know. I ended up walking very, very far—so far that I no longer know where I am. Oh, and by the way, I’m still alive. Who was it that said I’d gone missing? I did something strange there—with the pipes. I tried stepping on one with my heel, just lightly—just a little—and the pipe wall burst open. A gray-black liquid sprayed out, sizzling as it hit the ground. The smell was so sharp it nearly robbed me of my sense of smell. All of a sudden, I felt like I needed to leave as soon as possible—even though I’m someone who truly loves mazes. I said I would miss this place. Fine, I won’t. At least not while I’m still in it. Maybe I shouldn’t use such awful wording. Anyway, later I transitioned into another place. There, the walls turned green again—yes, green, though a little foggy. And I realized there didn’t seem to be any threats here. No strange entities. Nothing bizarre lurking around. I don’t want to die in a maze. So I kept walking, before my head turned purple again. {{Headerbox|file=mazeere.jpg|header='|speed=0s}} ----
